one_line_thesis: AbbVie is transitioning from Humira to Skyrizi/Rinvoq with durable owner cash (~$10/sh), but at ~$250 the base return (~7%) is below our accumulate hurdle; watch for a better entry.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
AbbVie is completing the handoff from Humira to Skyrizi and Rinvoq. FY2025 operating cash flow was $19.0 billion; after $1.2 billion of capital spending, owner cash is about $10.05 per share. Q1 2026 net revenues were $15.0 billion (+12.4% year over year), with Skyrizi U.S. sales $3.8 billion (+29%) and Rinvoq U.S. $1.4 billion (+20%).

At about $250 (Yahoo Finance, 2026-07-09), the Lawrence base case implies about 7.6% per year on normalized owner cash, below our 15% accumulate hurdle. The business has a stable durable advantage (Munger moat) on immunology franchises with patent protection...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

valuation_decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

timeline.label: Operating income moved +65.0% versus the prior period (9,137.0 to 15,075.0).
FDA submission for Skyrizi subcutaneous induction in moderately to severely active Crohn's disease
Positive Phase 3 AFFIRM topline for Skyrizi subcutaneous induction in Crohn's disease
FDA approved Venclexta plus acalabrutinib for previously untreated chronic lymphocytic leukemia
Voluntary U.S. government pricing agreement announced to advance access and affordability while protecting innovation investment
Raised quarterly dividend; declared $6.65 per share for FY2025
Humira U.S. biosimilar competition accelerated; U.S. Humira revenue fell to $3.1B (-57% YoY) in FY2025
Skyrizi and Rinvoq became primary growth engines; combined global immunology sales exceeded Humira trajectory
Closed Allergan acquisition; added aesthetics (Botox) and neuroscience portfolio; increased leverage
Spun off from Abbott Laboratories as independent biopharma company (NYSE: ABBV)

industry.structure: Global branded pharmaceuticals are concentrated among large incumbents with patent-protected biologics and specialty oral drugs; payers and governments exert pricing pressure but high switching costs persist in immunology.
industry.share_shift: AbbVie is gaining immunology share with Skyrizi and Rinvoq while losing Humira and Imbruvica share to biosimilars and competitive mechanisms.
industry.trend: Biosimilar penetration, U.S. price negotiation, and oral/advanced biologic competition are reshaping margins; migraine and aesthetics provide diversification.

top_claims.claim: Operating income moved +65.0% versus the prior period (9,137.0 to 15,075.0).

AbbVie is completing the handoff from Humira to Skyrizi and Rinvoq. FY2025 operating cash flow was $19.0 billion; after $1.2 billion of capital spending, owner cash is about $10.05 per share. Q1 2026 net revenues were $15.0 billion (+12.4% year over year), with Skyrizi U.S. sales $3.8 billion (+29%) and Rinvoq U.S. $1.
We expect **7.6%** per year over 7 years at **~$249.91** on **$10.05/sh** starting owner cash.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Immunology growth decelerates before debt maturities are comfortably refinanced, compressing owner cash just as Humira and Imbruvica erosion continue.
Leverage and interest expense: Net interest expense $2.6B FY2025; long-term obligations ~$65B (10-K).
Litigation and antitrust: Humira pricing lawsuits and product-liability matters can move non-cash reserves and settlement cash (10-K commitments; 10-Q legal proceedings).
